meta.title: Fixed Posture Modules (Premium)
meta.description: Curated, fixed modules for premium users, separate from the rotating daily plan. Each module is a hand-picked group of 4-5 exercises themed by time-of-day, body region, or context. Categories mirror the conventions used by leading posture/stretch apps (e.g. Bend's 'Posture Reset', 'Wake Up', 'Sleep'; NeckReset's 'Morning Unlock / Desk Break / Bedtime Release'; Stretch Reminder's region-based routines). All exercise_ids reference posture_exercises_db_v5.json. All exercises are equipment-free (wall/door only).
meta.ordering_principle: Within each module, exercises are sequenced warm-up/mobility first, then strengthening, then stretch/down-regulation, so the module flows sensibly start to finish.
meta.design_notes: Modules intentionally repeat some exercises across themes (e.g. chin tuck appears in several) because a fixed module is a self-contained product, unlike the rotating daily plan which avoids repeats. The daily-plan cooldown/family logic does NOT apply inside fixed modules.

modules.name: Morning Unlock
modules.tagline: Gentle mobility to undo overnight stiffness and start the day tall.
modules.rationale: Morning routines in the category lean on gentle mobility rather than hard strengthening, to loosen up a stiff body and set posture for the day.

modules.name: Desk Escape
modules.tagline: Discreet, mostly seated/standing moves to counter a long sitting block.
modules.rationale: The most common context module across apps: short, office-friendly moves targeting the neck, upper back, and hip flexors that suffer most from sitting. Chosen to need no floor.

modules.name: Bedtime Release
modules.tagline: Slow, long-hold stretches to unwind tension before sleep.
modules.rationale: Evening modules use gentle, longer-hold stretches and down-regulation rather than strengthening, mirroring Bend's 'Sleep' and NeckReset's 'Bedtime Release'.

modules.name: Tech Neck Fix
modules.tagline: Target forward head posture and neck tension from screens and phones.
modules.rationale: A region module focused on the neck: combines deep neck flexor strengthening (the most-evidenced FHP intervention) with the tight muscles that need releasing.

modules.name: Shoulder Un-Rounding
modules.tagline: Open the chest and strengthen the upper back to pull shoulders back.
modules.rationale: Rounded-shoulder correction pairs pec stretching with scapular retractor and serratus strengthening, the combination shown effective for forward shoulder posture.

modules.name: Upright Upper Back
modules.tagline: Mobilize and strengthen the mid-back against a hunched thoracic curve.
modules.rationale: Kyphosis-specific module combining thoracic extension mobility with extensor strengthening (prone cobra, superman), the approach shown to reduce kyphosis angle.

modules.name: Lower Back & Core Power
modules.tagline: Build the deep core and pelvic control that protect the lower back.
modules.rationale: Maps to the near-universal 'lower back' module, built around the core-stability and pelvic-tilt strengthening shown to improve lumbopelvic posture.

modules.name: Hip Flexor Reset
modules.tagline: Release tight hip flexors and fire up the glutes to neutralize pelvic tilt.
modules.rationale: Hips are a staple region module (Bend's 'Hips'). This pairs hip flexor release with glute strengthening, the stretch+strengthen combo for anterior pelvic tilt.

modules.name: Gamer Reboot
modules.tagline: Undo the neck-forward, shoulders-in, hips-locked posture that gaming locks in.
modules.rationale: Gaming drives a compound posture pattern: forward head, rounded shoulders, tight pecs, and hip flexor shortening from long static sitting. This module hits all four with strengthen-first ordering.

modules.name: Full-Body Alignment
modules.tagline: One exercise per region for a complete head-to-hips posture tune-up.
modules.rationale: Mirrors the 'Full Body' module every leading app offers. Hits all six body regions once, the multi-region comprehensive approach shown most effective in meta-analysis.

modules.name: Seated Neck Relief
modules.tagline: Five desk-friendly minutes to clear tension from your neck and jaw.
modules.rationale: A fully seated sequence targeting the lateral flexors, scalenes, and upper traps — the muscles that shorten during prolonged forward head posture. No floor work required.

modules.name: Chest & Spine Opener
modules.tagline: Undo the forward-collapse posture that builds up over hours of sitting.
modules.rationale: Pairs anterior chest stretching with thoracic extension to counter flexion-dominated posture. Ends with rotation to restore full three-dimensional spinal movement.

modules.name: Rotation Flow
modules.tagline: Restore the rotational freedom that desk work slowly locks away.
modules.rationale: Pure thoracic rotation from three positions — quadruped, side-lying, and upright — to systematically address stiffness at every segment of the thoracic spine.

modules.name: Glute Activation
modules.tagline: Re-engage the muscles your chair has been switching off all day.
modules.rationale: Activates all three glute heads and the hip abductors, then stretches the antagonist hip flexors. The classic combination for reversing anterior pelvic tilt from prolonged sitting.

modules.name: Shoulder Stability
modules.tagline: Build the serratus and lower trap strength that keeps your shoulders in place.
modules.rationale: Focused on serratus anterior and lower trapezius — the two muscles most responsible for scapular upward rotation and the prevention of shoulder impingement.

modules.name: Shoulder Blade Series
modules.tagline: The lower trap and rhomboid protocol used in shoulder rehab clinics worldwide.
modules.rationale: The Y-T-W-I protocol loads each region of the lower and middle trapezius in a shortened position — the hardest, most direct way to build the postural strength that holds shoulders back passively all day.

modules.name: Side Body Strength
modules.tagline: Build the lateral stability system that holds your spine against gravity.
modules.rationale: Trains the lateral subsystem — glute medius, QL, and obliques — alongside the external rotators. Side plank and unilateral glute work challenge the hip to resist lateral pelvic drop under load.

modules.name: Deep Neck Protocol
modules.tagline: Strengthen the deep cervical muscles that forward head posture gradually shuts off.
modules.rationale: Progressively loads the longus colli and longus capitis — the deep neck flexors — through four variations of chin tuck, finishing with posterior neck strengthening for balanced cervical stability.

modules.name: Dynamic Core
modules.tagline: Anti-rotation and anti-extension patterns that build real-world spine protection.
modules.rationale: Progresses through the anti-gravity challenge spectrum: floor-level (dead bug, bird dog), frontal plane (side plank), dynamic with arm dissociation (plank shoulder taps), and upright (standing bird dog).

modules.name: Balance & Body Sense
modules.tagline: Challenge the proprioceptive system that tells your brain where your body is in space.
modules.rationale: Single-leg stability with progressive challenge: static hold, multi-directional reach, loaded hip patterns, and upright coordination. Proprioceptive training here improves the automatic postural responses that prevent chronic spinal loading.
